title: reel:generate-storyboard args
description: Split a scene (or the whole episode) into multiple shots and write scene.shots[] with timeline placeholders. The workbench consumes the queue, runs its director storyboard engine in-browser, and lays the shots out on the timeline for preview. The workbench must be open. This is the FIRST step of per-node production: do it before generating per-shot keyframes/videos.

scope.description: 'scene' (default): split only the node given by sceneId (focused, cheap). 'all': run the batch engine over the whole episode (cross-scene consistency loopback).

sceneId.description: Required when scope='scene': the node (key in scenario.scenes) to storyboard.

scenarioId.description: Optional target scenario id. Defaults to the workbench's active scenario.

force.description: Re-split and CLEAN the node first. Set force=true whenever the user asks to '重新生成 / 重做 / 重拆 / 重新分镜 / redo / regenerate' a node that already has shots — this replaces the existing shots/dialogue timing on the timeline with a fresh decomposition (old videos/keyframes are NOT deleted; they are archived to the asset library and stay retrievable). The workbench will ask the user to confirm before replacing existing content. Omit/false for a first-time storyboard or to keep author-polished shots.
